source_note: Paraphrased from the official IELTS Band Descriptors (Public Version, British Council / IDP / Cambridge) — same convention as skill_taxonomy_*.json. Keyed by section → criterion_id → integer band. Used by Backward Design: every Tutor activity anchors to the learner's NEXT band descriptor.

descriptors.4: Responds to the task only in a minimal way; position unclear; ideas are difficult to identify and few are developed.
descriptors.5: Addresses the task only partially; expresses a position but development is not always clear; some ideas are limited or not well supported.
descriptors.6: Addresses all parts of the task although some parts more fully than others; presents a relevant position although conclusions may be unclear or repetitive.
descriptors.7: Addresses all parts of the task; presents a clear position throughout; presents, extends and supports main ideas, though there may be a tendency to over-generalise.
descriptors.8: Sufficiently addresses all parts of the task; presents a well-developed response with relevant, extended and supported ideas.
descriptors.9: Fully addresses all parts of the task; presents a fully developed position with relevant, fully extended and well-supported ideas.

descriptors.4: Presents information and ideas but these are not arranged coherently; uses some basic cohesive devices inaccurately or repetitively; may not paragraph at all.
descriptors.5: Presents information with some organisation but there may be a lack of overall progression; makes inadequate, inaccurate or over-use of cohesive devices; paragraphing may be inadequate.
descriptors.6: Arranges information coherently with clear overall progression; uses cohesive devices effectively but cohesion within or between sentences may be faulty or mechanical; paragraphing is used but not always logically.
descriptors.7: Logically organises information; clear progression throughout; uses a range of cohesive devices appropriately although there may be some under- or over-use; presents a clear central topic within each paragraph.
descriptors.8: Sequences information and ideas logically; manages all aspects of cohesion well; uses paragraphing sufficiently and appropriately.
descriptors.9: Uses cohesion in such a way that it attracts no attention; skilfully manages paragraphing.

descriptors.4: Uses only basic vocabulary which may be used repetitively; limited control of word formation and spelling; errors may cause strain for the reader.
descriptors.5: Uses a limited range of vocabulary minimally adequate for the task; noticeable errors in spelling and word formation that may cause some difficulty.
descriptors.6: Uses an adequate range of vocabulary for the task; attempts less common vocabulary but with some inaccuracy; some spelling and word-formation errors that do not impede communication.
descriptors.7: Uses a sufficient range of vocabulary with some flexibility and precision; uses less common items with some awareness of style and collocation; occasional errors in word choice or spelling.
descriptors.8: Uses a wide range of vocabulary fluently and flexibly; skilfully uses uncommon items; rare errors in spelling or word formation.
descriptors.9: Uses a wide range of vocabulary with very natural and sophisticated control; rare minor slips only.

descriptors.4: Uses only a very limited range of structures; some structures accurate but errors predominate and punctuation is often faulty.
descriptors.5: Uses only a limited range of structures; attempts complex sentences but these tend to be less accurate than simple sentences; frequent errors may cause some difficulty.
descriptors.6: Uses a mix of simple and complex sentence forms; makes some errors in grammar and punctuation but they rarely reduce communication.
descriptors.7: Uses a variety of complex structures; produces frequent error-free sentences; has good control of grammar and punctuation with only a few errors.
descriptors.8: Uses a wide range of structures; the majority of sentences are error-free; makes only very occasional errors or inappropriacies.
descriptors.9: Uses a wide range of structures with full flexibility and accuracy; rare minor slips only.

descriptors.4: Frequently fails to locate stated information; scanning is slow and often anchored to the wrong paragraph.
descriptors.5: Locates explicitly stated information when wording closely matches the text; misses answers that require moving beyond keyword matching.
descriptors.6: Locates most stated information including simply paraphrased detail; occasionally caught by distractor placement.
descriptors.7: Locates information reliably even when paraphrased; manages True/False/Not Given distinctions with only occasional slips.
descriptors.8: Locates information quickly and accurately across dense text; rarely misled by traps or near-miss distractors.
descriptors.9: Locates any stated detail rapidly and with near-perfect accuracy under full exam timing.

descriptors.4: Grasps only surface topic; cannot reliably identify main ideas or follow an argument across paragraphs.
descriptors.5: Identifies main ideas in straightforward text; inference and writer's-purpose questions are frequently missed.
descriptors.6: Follows argument structure and identifies main ideas reliably; draws simple inferences but misses subtler implied meaning.
descriptors.7: Draws valid inferences from implied information; distinguishes claims from evidence with only occasional errors.
descriptors.8: Handles fine inference, writer stance and nuance with rare errors, including in above-level texts.
descriptors.9: Complete command of literal and implied meaning at speed.

descriptors.4: Unknown words regularly block comprehension; little evidence of using context to deduce meaning.
descriptors.5: Deduces meaning of common words from context; paraphrase recognition is unreliable.
descriptors.6: Recognises common paraphrase and synonym substitution; deduces most unfamiliar word meanings from context.
descriptors.7: Recognises sophisticated paraphrase reliably; unfamiliar vocabulary rarely affects answer accuracy.
descriptors.8: Handles low-frequency vocabulary and idiomatic paraphrase with rare errors.
descriptors.9: Vocabulary poses no barrier at any text level.

descriptors.4: Little awareness of how texts are organised; cannot use paragraph structure to navigate.
descriptors.5: Recognises basic organisation (introduction, body, conclusion) but cannot use it to locate answers efficiently.
descriptors.6: Uses paragraph topic sentences and discourse markers to navigate; matching-headings tasks remain error-prone.
descriptors.7: Uses text organisation strategically — predicts where answer types will appear; handles matching-headings reliably.
descriptors.8: Navigates dense, unconventionally structured texts with rare errors.
descriptors.9: Complete structural command of any text type at exam pace.

descriptors.4: Misses most specific details; cannot reliably decode names, numbers or connected speech at natural pace.
descriptors.5: Catches details in slow, clear audio; weak forms, spelling of names and fast stretches cause frequent misses.
descriptors.6: Catches most stated details; fast passages, corrections and distractor traps still cause errors.
descriptors.7: Catches specific detail reliably in single-play conditions including most paraphrased detail.
descriptors.8: Detail retrieval is accurate across accents and speeds with rare gaps.
descriptors.9: Near-perfect detail retrieval at any natural speed.

descriptors.4: Follows only isolated words and phrases; cannot state what the recording was about.
descriptors.5: Follows the main idea of slow, clear audio; loses the thread when speakers digress or correct themselves.
descriptors.6: Follows main ideas and speaker purpose reliably; subtle shifts in topic or attitude are sometimes missed.
descriptors.7: Tracks main ideas, speaker intent and attitude through corrections and digressions in single-play conditions.
descriptors.8: Grasps gist, purpose and attitude with rare errors even in dense academic monologues.
descriptors.9: Complete grasp of literal and implied meaning at exam pace.

descriptors.4: Cannot infer unstated meaning; interprets everything literally.
descriptors.5: Draws simple inferences when strongly signalled; misses implied opinion and relationship between speakers.
descriptors.6: Infers speaker opinion and context reliably when moderately signalled; subtle implication still missed.
descriptors.7: Infers unstated meaning, opinion and speaker relationships reliably under single-play conditions.
descriptors.8: Handles subtle implication, irony and attitude with rare errors.
descriptors.9: Complete inferential command at exam pace.

descriptors.4: A single missed answer derails the rest of the section; no question preview or prediction.
descriptors.5: Previews questions but predictions are vague; recovers from misses slowly, losing subsequent answers.
descriptors.6: Predicts answer types from question preview; abandons missed answers and re-anchors within a question or two.
descriptors.7: Predicts content and language before listening; recovers immediately from misses using signposting language.
descriptors.8: Strategic control is automatic; misses are rare and never cost subsequent answers.
descriptors.9: Complete strategic control throughout the section.

descriptors.4: Speaks with long pauses; limited ability to link simple sentences; frequent repetition and self-correction.
descriptors.5: Maintains flow of speech but uses repetition, self-correction and slow speech to keep going; over-uses certain connectives.
descriptors.6: Speaks at length though may lose coherence at times; uses a range of connectives though not always appropriately.
descriptors.7: Speaks at length without noticeable effort; some hesitation is language-related rather than content-related.
descriptors.8: Fluent with only rare repetition or self-correction; hesitation is content-related only; topics developed coherently.
descriptors.9: Fluent with only very rare, content-related hesitation; fully coherent and extended discourse.

descriptors.4: Basic vocabulary sufficient only for familiar topics; frequent word-choice errors.
descriptors.5: Manages to talk about familiar and unfamiliar topics but with limited flexibility; attempts paraphrase with mixed success.
descriptors.6: Wide enough vocabulary to discuss topics at length; uses paraphrase effectively; some inappropriate word choices.
descriptors.7: Uses vocabulary flexibly including some less common and idiomatic items; some awareness of style and collocation.
descriptors.8: Wide vocabulary used fluently to convey precise meaning; skilful use of less common and idiomatic items.
descriptors.9: Full flexibility and precision in all contexts; sustained use of accurate and idiomatic language.

descriptors.4: Basic sentence forms only; frequent errors even in simple structures.
descriptors.5: Produces basic forms with reasonable accuracy; complex structures attempted but usually contain errors.
descriptors.6: Mix of simple and complex structures with limited flexibility; errors recur with complex structures but rarely impede communication.
descriptors.7: Range of complex structures used with some flexibility; frequent error-free sentences though some errors persist.
descriptors.8: Wide range of structures used flexibly; majority of sentences error-free; only occasional slips.
descriptors.9: Full range of structures used naturally and appropriately; errors are rare and minor.

descriptors.4: Mispronunciations are frequent and cause strain; limited control of stress and intonation.
descriptors.5: Generally intelligible but mispronunciation of individual words or sounds reduces clarity at times.
descriptors.6: Can generally be understood throughout; uses some pronunciation features but control is mixed.
descriptors.7: Easy to understand throughout; sustained use of stress, rhythm and intonation with occasional lapses.
descriptors.8: Easy to understand; wide range of pronunciation features used flexibly; accent has minimal effect.
descriptors.9: Effortless to understand; full and precise control of all pronunciation features.
